content_preview.title: Temporal Adds Serverless Workers to Its Durable Execution Platform at Replay 2026
content_preview.summary: Temporal unveils Serverless Workers for AWS Lambda at Replay 2026, letting teams run durable workflows on pay-per-invocation compute with no cluster management.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Temporal, the open-source durable execution platform, used its annual Replay 2026 conference to unveil Serverless Workers, a pre-release feature that lets teams deploy Temporal Workers directly to AWS Lambda without provisioning or managing long-running compute clusters. The announcement came as part of a broad wave of product launches at the three-day event, which drew more than 2,000 developers to [Moscone South in San Francisco on May 5-7, 2026](https://replay.temporal.io/).

## ...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article at 928 words (within the 400–1200 range). Clear section hierarchy covering the flagship announcement, related features, GA announcements, and customer scale data. Analysis section provides meaningful context without editorializing.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 sources fetched successfully (HTTP 200) and read from disk. source-0 (source-0.html.gz): Temporal blog by Brandon Chavis confirming all Serverless Workers specifics — quotes, SDK support, Lambda 15-min limit, Google Cloud Run roadmap, pay-per-invocation model — all verified verbatim. source-1 (source-1.html.gz): Meagan Speare's Replay 2026 product announcements — confirms Workflow Streams Public Preview for Python, Google ADK GA, OpenAI Agents SDK GA, Worker Versioning GA, Multi-region/Multi-cloud Replication GA with 20-minute RTO, Task Queue Priority & Fairness GA, OpenMetrics GA, SCIM GA, Capacity Modes GA, Nexus Python GA + TypeScript/.NET Public Preview, Worker Status UI heartbeat 60-second interval. All confirmed. source-2 (source-2.html.gz): Replay conference recap by Jap Singh — confirms Netflix 150+ teams, 500+ namespaces, 7+ billion actions/month; Cursor 50M actions/day, 7M+ workflows, 99%+ success rate, one-third PRs from cloud agents; OpenAI 60x growth; Venkat Venkataramani quote verified verbatim. NOTE: The article attributes a direct quote to Maxim Fateev — 'Production applications require a certain level of resiliency, scale, and observability' — to his keynote remarks, but this exact quote does not appear in source-2 or any of the 5 snapshots. source-2 only mentions meeting Fateev briefly in passing with no keynote quote. source-3 (source-3.html.gz): Series D press release confirming $300M at $5B valuation, Samar Abbas CEO quote verified verbatim. source-4 (source-4.html.gz): replay.temporal.io conference site confirming May 5-7 dates and Moscone South location.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: The vast majority of claims are well-sourced and accurately stated. One unsourced direct quote: Maxim Fateev's 'Production applications require a certain level of resiliency, scale, and observability' is attributed to his keynote remarks but does not appear in any source snapshot. This is a subordinate supporting claim in the 'Customer Scale' section, not in the headline, summary, or lead. All other specifics — SDK names, dates, percentages, company names, feature states (GA/Preview/Pre-release) — are verified against cited sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism or editorializing. Analysis section is appropriately measured.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of Temporal or Replay 2026 found. New topic, no duplicate.
concerns: Direct quote attributed to Maxim Fateev ('Production applications require a certain level of resiliency, scale, and observability') does not appear in any of the 5 cited source snapshots. The source-2 blog post mentions Fateev in passing but contains no keynote quote. This is a fabricated or paraphrased quote presented as verbatim in quote marks.

All 5 sources are on temporal.io / replay.temporal.io, which is not yet in config/source_allowlist.txt. However, this is appropriate primary-source coverage: the article is about Temporal's own announcements, so the company's official blog and press release page are the correct authoritative sources. Adding temporal.io to the allowlist is recommended.
recommendations: File a corrections record documenting the unsourced Fateev quote.
Add temporal.io and replay.temporal.io to config/source_allowlist.txt as primary sources for official Temporal announcements.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality News article with accurate, well-sourced reporting on Temporal's Replay 2026 announcements. The single issue is a direct quote attributed to Maxim Fateev that cannot be verified in any source snapshot. All other facts, quotes, and feature details are confirmed. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS — the Fateev quote should be corrected in the public record.
